differentials.name: Amelanotic pattern (38%) ◊  Pattern similar to other brain neoplasm
differentials.raw: Amelanotic pattern (38%) ◊  Pattern similar to other brain neoplasm

context: Prevalence:   39% at autopsy Location:   cerebrum > cerebellum; usually multiple lesions √  tendency for hemorrhage √  paramagnetic property of melanin → T1-shortening effect: √  hyperintense relative to cortex on T1WI √  iso- / hypointense relative to cortex on T2WI Cause:   free radicals in melanin + blood products ◊  NO clear consensus on contribution of paramagnetic effect of blood products versus melanin! √  hypo- / isointense on T1WI √  hyper- / isointense on T2WI √  isointense on T1WI √  hyperintense on T2WI Prognosis:  median survival of 113 days after discovery DDx:  primary malignant
